title: Functional Calibration for Trunk and Lower Limb Fixed Inertial Sensors
author: Benedikt Fasel
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.itrcem6

steps.title: Slow squats with knee, hip, trunk, head flexion
steps.instruction: Slow squats with knee, hip, trunk, head flexion. Arms are parallel to the leg. Perform the flexion movements until the fingers reach the ankles. Perform the movement three times. Squats front view a Squats lateral view a

steps.title: Slow trunk rotations around the vertical axis with hips fixed
steps.instruction: Slow trunk rotations around the vertical axis with hips fixed. Arms hold a ski pole lying horizontally behind the neck. Head turns with the trunk. Perform the movement three times where the rotation starts by looking to the right. Trunk rotations frontal view a Trunk rotations lateral view a

steps.title: Slow hip ad/abductions of first the right leg
steps.instruction: Slow hip ad/abductions of first the right leg. Control balance using the ski poles. Right heel is positioned in-line with left toe. Keep knee straight through the entire movement. Perform the movement of slow hip abduction and adduction three times. Then perform the same with the left leg. Note: Athletes have the tendency to externally rotate their hip during the hip abduction. The toes should always remain pointing forwards, with no external hip rotation. Train the correct movement a few times prior to perform the entire functional calibration procedure. Hip ad/abduction frontal view a Hip ad/abduction lateral view a

steps.title: Stand upright with knees slightly flexed
steps.instruction: Stand upright with knees slightly flexed. Keep equal weight on both feet. Look straight to the front. Stand still for 10 seconds. Upright frontal view a Upright lateral view a
